topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

The first draft landed on my desk, and a strange, cold knot tightened in my stomach. I picked up the manuscript, the familiar, intoxicating rhythm of the prose immediately washing over me. It was *his* voice, the very cadence I lived for. My breath hitched as I scanned the opening chapter, and then, a detail. A specific metaphor, a turn of phrase that had been my personal signature for years, was lifted, whole and unadorned, from a chapter in his latest published work. My hands began to tremble, the paper suddenly feeling slick under my fingertips. I reread the passage, my eyes darting back and forth, searching for any flaw, any sign of fabrication, but the resemblance was undeniable, a perfect, damning mirror. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck. I needed to know. I needed to confront this sudden, sickening realization that the foundation of my passion had been built on sand.

[story 2]

I paced the length of my small apartment, the worn floorboards protesting beneath my hurried steps. My jaw was tight, my knuckles white where I gripped the phone. Every notification pinged with an urgency that made my pulse jump against my ribs. I had seen the article—a scathing piece dissecting the structural similarities between my unpublished short story collection and the celebrated author’s recent novel. My mind raced, a frantic hummingbird trapped in a cage. I pulled up the comparison documents, my fingers flying across the keyboard, searching for the precise textual evidence. When I found it, the air left my lungs in a sharp gasp. It wasn't just similar; it was a direct appropriation. A profound sense of violation washed over me, swiftly morphing into a burning need for resolution. I had to see him. I had to address this imbalance immediately.

[story 3]

A restless energy thrummed beneath my skin, a restless vibration that made sitting still impossible. I kept tapping my pen against my thigh, a rapid, insistent rhythm. The stack of proofs felt heavy, oppressive, each page a physical manifestation of the betrayal I had just uncovered. I leaned back in my chair, closing my eyes, trying to steady the sudden tremor in my hands. When I opened them, the familiar landscape of my beloved author’s writing seemed suddenly tainted, every cherished sentence now carrying a shadow of deceit. A fierce determination sparked within me. I didn't want to simply process this; I wanted to dismantle it. I began drafting an email, the words forming with sharp, unwavering precision, every keystroke fueled by a singular, powerful drive to expose the truth.
